title: The Garden
content:
Many things the garden shows,
    And pleased I stray
    From tree to tree
    Watching the white pear-bloom,
    Bee-infested quince or plum.
    I could walk days, years, away
    Till the slow ripening, secular tree
    Had reached its fruiting-time,
    Nor think it long.



    Solar insect on the wing
    In the garden murmuring,
    Soothing with thy summer horn
    Swains by winter pinched and worn.
meaning: About: Ralph Waldo Emerson was an American essayist, philosopher, poet, and leader of the Transcendentalist movement in the early 19th century. His teachings directly influenced the growing New Thought movement of the mid 1800s.
